name: CDIF
tagline: A 'lingua franca' for FAIR implementation across domain boundaries.

bookCard.title: The CDIF Book
bookCard.description: Read the foundational guidelines and implementation patterns online.

background.title: Background & History
background.lead: The Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework represents more than a decade of international efforts to streamline data exchange across scientific fields.

evolution.title: The Evolution of CDIF
paragraphs: CDIF was born out of the recognition that domain-specific standards, while essential for their own communities, often fail to provide the necessary metadata for effective reuse in multi-disciplinary research. By identifying the common core of metadata needed for discovery, access, and integration, CDIF provides a 'lingua franca' that bridges these gaps.
Today, the framework continues to evolve under the guidance of CODATA and a growing global community of FAIR data experts and practitioners.

projects.title: Projects & Partners
projects.lead: CDIF is being adopted and validated by a diverse range of international projects and research infrastructures.

membership.title: Membership & Working Groups
membership.lead: Participation in CDIF is driven by our specialized Working Groups. Members typically represent organizations or major research projects committed to cross-domain interoperability.

join.title: Interested in Joining?
join.description: We welcome contributions from individuals and organizations across all scientific domains. Express your interest in joining a working group today.

news.title: Latest News & Events
news.lead: Stay up to date with the latest developments in the Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework community.

tools.title: Essential Tools
tools.lead: A curated collection of core tools designed to support the implementation and validation of CDIF across diverse platforms.

footer.description: The Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework (CDIF) is a set of practical, implementation-level guidelines designed to improve data management practices and unlock cross-domain data reuse.
footer.attribution: A CODATA Supported Initiative.
